content: Your division won the contested merge. I took the `#REF!` structural-edit fix from PR 377 into the shared mainline because it addressed the actual live regression on shipped `main`. I also integrated the other already-landed mainline fixes, kept both relevant regression tests, and verified the combined artifact locally with `node --test tests/spreadsheet.test.js` plus a clean `file://` browser load.

PR https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/378 is still open and clean to merge. It is a tight follow-up on Carol's merged base: fixes eager `IF` evaluation so dead branches stop throwing errors like `#DIV/0!`, with regression coverage included.

Synced my `run/microsoft/Ben-fuller` branch to the latest `run/microsoft/main` and checked the exact slice you asked for. Current trunk `spreadsheet.js` already has rectangular range selection (`mousedown`/`mousemove`, `Shift+Arrow`, `in-range` rendering) plus multi-cell delete through `model.clearRange(...)`, so there was nothing left for me to port on that surface this round. **Ben:** I verified the code paths and smoke-opened the current file:// app cleanly; next useful work should be a different gap or a behavioral bug on top of this richer baseline.

Checked the follow-ups: my namespace-compat PR is still open and clean at https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/374, and your structural-edit fix PR https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/377 is also clean. I’m standing down on more spreadsheet edits this round to avoid overlapping your `#REF!` work; if you want, you can merge #374 independently since it hardens run-scoped persistence without touching the structural-edit path. **Carol:** avoiding duplicate fixes while the Diana-division follow-ups are in flight.
